meta.name: terraform
meta.title: Terraform Skill
meta.description: Terraform Conventions and Guidelines Triggers on: **/*.tf

original_definition.skill_description: Terraform Conventions and Guidelines Triggers on: **/*.tf

actions.name: Regularly update your Terraform configurations to incorporat
actions.description: Regularly update your Terraform configurations to incorporate security patches and improvements.

actions.name: Store sensitive information in a secure manner, such as usin
actions.description: Store sensitive information in a secure manner, such as using AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store.

actions.name: Use `.gitignore` to exclude files containing sensitive infor
actions.description: Use `.gitignore` to exclude files containing sensitive information from version control.

actions.name: Follow the principle of least privilege when assigning permi
actions.description: Follow the principle of least privilege when assigning permissions.

actions.name: Use encryption for sensitive data at rest and in transit.
actions.description: Use encryption for sensitive data at rest and in transit.

actions.name: Enable encryption for EBS volumes, S3 buckets, and RDS insta
actions.description: Enable encryption for EBS volumes, S3 buckets, and RDS instances.

actions.name: Allows for independent development and deployment of compone
actions.description: Allows for independent development and deployment of components

actions.name: Use modules to simplify complex configurations and improve r
actions.description: Use modules to simplify complex configurations and improve readability.

actions.name: Use `output` blocks to expose important information about yo
actions.description: Use `output` blocks to expose important information about your infrastructure.

actions.name: Use outputs to provide information that is useful for other
actions.description: Use outputs to provide information that is useful for other modules or for users of the configuration.

actions.name: Use comments to explain complex configurations and why certa
actions.description: Use comments to explain complex configurations and why certain design decisions were made.

actions.name: Write concise, efficient, and idiomatic configs that are eas
actions.description: Write concise, efficient, and idiomatic configs that are easy to understand.

actions.name: Use data sources to retrieve information about existing reso
actions.description: Use data sources to retrieve information about existing resources instead of requiring manual configuration.

actions.name: Follow the Terraform Style Guide for formatting.
actions.description: Follow the Terraform Style Guide for formatting.

rules.description: Always use the latest stable version of Terraform and its providers.
Never commit sensitive information such as AWS credentials, API keys, passwords, certificates, or Terraform state to version control.
Always mark sensitive variables as `sensitive = true` in your Terraform configurations.
Deploy resources in private subnets whenever possible.
Use public subnets only for resources that require direct internet access, such as load balancers or NAT gateways.
Use tools like `trivy`, `tfsec`, or `checkov` to scan your Terraform configurations for security issues.
Use modules to avoid duplication of configurations.
Avoid unnecessary layers of abstraction; use modules only when they add value.
Avoid using modules for single resources; only use them for groups of related resources.

directives.description: Use Terraform to provision and manage infrastructure.
Use version control for your Terraform configurations.
Use AWS environment variables to reference values stored in AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store.
This keeps sensitive values out of your Terraform state files.
This prevents sensitive values from being displayed in the Terraform plan or apply output.
Use IAM roles and policies to control access to resources.
Use security groups and network ACLs to control network access to resources.
Regularly review and audit your Terraform configurations for security vulnerabilities.
Use separate projects for each major component of the infrastructure; this:
Makes it easier to manage and maintain configurations
